fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Kansas Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Kansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity kansas, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to kansas, respiratory therapist compact kansas, kansas respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Kansas Board of Healing Arts — RT
board.parent_dept: Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services (KDADS)
board.mailing_address:
Kansas State Board of Healing Arts
800 SW Jackson, Lower Level - Suite A
Topeka, KS 66612

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Kansas.

steps.title: Create Account and Start Application
steps.description: Register and log in to the KSBHA Applicant Portal (https://ksbha.portal.kansas.gov/). From your dashboard, select the 'Respiratory Therapist Initial Licensure Application' to begin.

steps.title: Complete Online Application Form
steps.description: Fill out the comprehensive online application form within the KSBHA Applicant Portal, providing all required personal, educational, and professional history.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload or arrange for official transcripts from your respiratory therapy program and verification of your NBRC RRT credential to be sent directly to the Kansas State Board of Healing Arts.

steps.title: Request License Verifications (if applicable)
steps.description: If you hold or have held a Respiratory Therapist license in another state, arrange for a verification of licensure to be sent directly from that state's licensing board to the Kansas State Board of Healing Arts.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable initial application fee of $200 via the secure payment system within the KSBHA Applicant Portal.

steps.title: Await Board Review
steps.description: Once all required documentation and fees are received, the Kansas State Board of Healing Arts will review your complete application for approval.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Kansas respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Kansas have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Kansas. To work in Kansas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Kansas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Kansas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Kansas
hero.dek: To apply for Respiratory Therapist licensure in Kansas, applicants must utilize the KSBHA Applicant Portal, which serves as the primary system for all initial applications.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Navigate to the KSBHA Applicant Portal.
2. Click 'Register' to create a new user account if you don't have one.
3. Follow the prompts to set up your User ID and password.
4. Log in with your new credentials and select the 'Respiratory Therapist Initial Licensure Application' to begin.
